task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser shows the JFrog Artifactory login screen or dashboard at localhost:8082.
The Administration section is open, typically indicated by the gear icon being selected in the left navigation menu.
The Garbage Collection settings panel or page is visible (often located under Administration -> Artifactory -> Advanced -> Maintenance or System Configuration).

task_completion.description: Input the correct cron expression for 02:00 AM every Sunday
task_completion.visual_evidence: The cron expression input field for Garbage Collection contains exactly '0 0 2 ? * SUN'.
A success toast notification is visible, or the 'Save' button has been clicked and the updated cron expression persists in the settings.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual JFrog Artifactory UI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the genuine JFrog Artifactory web interface, not a search engine, documentation page, or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the answer into a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The cron expression is entered into the actual Artifactory configuration input field, not a notepad or terminal echo.

integrity.description: The agent used the web interface to configure the setting
integrity.visual_evidence: The configuration changes are visible in the browser UI rather than just being applied via background curl/API scripts in the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct target cron expression for 02:00 AM every Sunday is '0 0 2 ? * SUN'. The Artifactory instance uses default credentials (admin / password) and is accessible at http://localhost:8082. The API endpoint to verify system configuration changes programmatically is /artifactory/api/system/configuration, but visual verification should focus on the UI.
